behaviors.description: The wait time between retries doubles with each consecutive failure, up to a configured maximum.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This can be validated as pure backoff math (attempt count -> delay with cap) at one object/function boundary. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: No OS/network/subprocess is required; fakes do not hide this failure mode. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: Not applicable. Rejection-gate: Rejected Integration because the behavior text is strictly the delay formula and cap, with no required module handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Test a backoff calculator directly with consecutive failure counts and config (base delay, multiplier=2, max delay), asserting exact sequence and cap enforcement.

behaviors.description: The retry logic correctly identifies a rate-limit response and computes the appropriate wait time before the next attempt.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Failure is at a concrete cross-module contract: HTTP/error classification output must correctly drive retry scheduler delay selection. Unit of either classifier or calculator alone can miss mapping bugs. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network needed; stub response objects suffice, so not System. (b)(c)(d) no AI/agentic dependency. Rejection-gate: Rejected System because behavior does not require real HTTP transport behavior, only response interpretation + scheduling logic.
behaviors.test_description: Wire response classifier + retry policy + orchestrator with stubbed API responses (429/headers/body variants). Assert classifier flags rate-limit and orchestrator schedules expected computed delay.

behaviors.description: After the wait, the client actually resends the original request and returns the successful result to the caller transparently.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Real failure boundary is orchestration handoff: timer/backoff completion -> resend original request payload -> resolve caller with retried result. Unit tests of pieces can miss lost/mutated request state. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) fake timers + stub transport reproduce failure; no real network/subprocess required. (b)(c)(d) no AI/agentic dependency. Rejection-gate: Rejected System because behavior text is about resend semantics, not real network timing pathologies.
behaviors.test_description: Use fake clock and stub transport: first call returns rate-limit, second returns success. Assert second outbound call matches original request exactly and `call()` resolves with successful payload without exposing retry internals.

behaviors.description: After the configured maximum number of retries, the client returns a typed `QuotaExhaustedError` rather than a generic error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Contract spans retry counter/state machine and error-mapping layer; wrong boundary wiring can surface generic errors. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network required; deterministic stub failures trigger path. (b)(c)(d) no AI/agentic dependency. Rejection-gate: Rejected System because behavior is error typing after retry exhaustion, which in-process stubs can fully exercise.
behaviors.test_description: Configure max retries N, stub transport to always return rate-limit, run `call()`, and assert rejection is `QuotaExhaustedError` (type/class and key fields), not raw/generic error.

behaviors.description: The full retry chain — initial call, backoff retries, quota tracking — terminates correctly and does not loop indefinitely before surfacing the error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This is explicitly multi-module local orchestration (retry loop + backoff + quota tracker termination invariant). Unit tests of isolated components can miss loop-control coupling bugs. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) fake timers and stub transport can detect non-termination/extra attempts; no real OS/network mandatory. (b)(c)(d) no AI/agentic dependency. Rejection-gate: Rejected System because infinite-loop risk here is algorithmic state coupling, not infrastructure-specific behavior.
behaviors.test_description: With fake timers and always-rate-limited stub responses, execute `call()` and assert bounded attempts (initial + max retries), deterministic termination, and surfaced exhaustion error within expected virtual time.

behaviors.description: Requests issued concurrently from multiple async callers in the same process all draw from the same shared quota bucket.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Concrete cross-module invariant is shared-state coupling across concurrent `call()` invocations; per-caller isolation bug appears only when scheduler + quota store interact. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) can be reproduced with in-process concurrency and stubs; no real network/subprocess needed. (b)(c)(d) no AI/agentic dependency. Rejection-gate: Rejected System because behavior scope is same-process shared bucket semantics, not OS/network effects.
behaviors.test_description: Launch multiple concurrent `call()` promises against one library instance/account using stubbed API and controlled clock; assert a single shared quota counter/bucket governs all callers and aggregate allowed attempts match account quota rules.

behaviors.description: When multiple requests are waiting to be sent, they are dispatched in the order they were originally submitted.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Ordering bug lives at queue + dispatcher handoff boundary under contention; single-function unit tests miss real enqueue/dequeue interaction timing. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) deterministic in-process queue tests with fake timers are sufficient; no real OS/network required. (b)(c)(d) no AI/agentic dependency. Rejection-gate: Rejected System because FIFO dispatch correctness is internal scheduling logic, not external infrastructure behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Queue several requests while quota is unavailable, then advance time/release tokens and assert outbound dispatch order matches submission order exactly (FIFO) across async callers.
